name: ETH State Change Momentum Hold v1
edge_mechanism: state-change momentum after regime improvement and post-break hold
thesis: ETH 4h is the best primary lane for testing whether a local state change can be identified not by first breakout excitement but by break-hold behavior after regime conditions improve from dead chop. The edge is the post-break hold, not the breakout bar itself.
invalidation_condition: Abort if hold-confirmed state changes remain too sparse, or if post-break holds repeatedly fail back into the prior range before exits can pay costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A previously choppy market starts improving, breaks value, then holds above reclaimed structure long enough for a short continuation leg.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: A break that survives one bar of hold is more informative than the initial breakout print in chop-heavy conditions.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the cleanest lane for state-change tests because structural holds are visible without lower-timeframe noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The market may look improved briefly but still mean-revert back into range, making the hold too weak after fees.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries are dense enough but follow-through is weak, reduce runner ambition before tightening the hold filter.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Modest number of state-change continuations with first target doing most of the work and only occasional runner persistence.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h plus one cross-lane show credible density and positive post-cost continuation after confirmed holds.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sparse mirage, or repeated hold failures back into range.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; regime improvement and hold logic are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad hold-confirmation grammar rather than exact breakout candle dependence

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for structural hold after state change.
validation_basket.continue_if: Hold-confirmed breaks occur often enough and continue after costs.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Holds fail back into range or density collapses.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Break-hold patterns that produce short continuation legs after value shifts.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same state-change mechanism survives on the other major.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows similar break-hold continuation structure.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly cleaner but fewer state-change holds than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same hold grammar survives on the faster adjacent lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: 1h preserves density without degenerating into noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More frequent but smaller state-change continuations.
